mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 when UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and a declining federal funds rate indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative policy, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during high inflation periods, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of expansionary INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal economic expansion and higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 315, persistent inflation forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of threshold-crossing months, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and declines in the unemployment rate indicate synchronized strength in the construction sector and labor market, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when tested against a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend reflects broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate profits, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when financial conditions remain accommodative.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns during periods of tight financial conditions, or the 60-day forward return correlation with GDPC1 growth falls below 0.10.
